text: … Hours more passed, but it wasn’t until the first gray light of dawn touched the sky that Lara faltered, legs giving out beneath her . Ronin held onto her, dipping down to scoop her into his arms without missing a step. “A little farther. There’s a place ahead where we can shelter for a time .” Her lead lolled against him, and she turned into his warmth, throwing an arm over his shoulder. The only response she could manage was a weak nod . She listened to the water sloshing in his bag, let herself be lulled by the steady rhythm of his strides. He was moving faster now that he was carrying her, but his pace didn’t jostle her. It was comforting, focusing only on his heat, on the solidness of his body; her own aches, pains, and weariness were forgotten for a while . * * * A loud pop startled Lara awake. Her eyes went wide, the flickering flames before her blurry until she blinked away her grogginess. She lay on the ground, her head propped on something firm and warm. Brow furrowed, she turned her head to look up at Ronin. Her head was resting on his thigh. He’d removed her scarf, but his coat was over her like a blanket . “You’re safe,” he said, settling a hand on her shoulder . “Water,” she croaked . Ronin twisted the cap off her canteen and held it to her. She cupped the bottom, tilting it back as she lifted her head and drank deeply. The water was heavenly to her parched throat. When she’d had her fill, she pushed it away and dropped her head back into his lap . “ Thanks .” “You should have been drinking more often while we walked .” “Didn’t want to slow you down any more than I already did.” She met his gaze. “Sorry I fell asleep .” “You’ll slow me down a lot more if you get dehydrated, Lara.” He pressed his palm to her cheek. “My goal is to keep you safe, not walk you to death .” She placed her hand over his and smiled. “Could’ve fooled me .” “Some of this will get easier. Your body will grow accustomed to the exertion .” “And how do you know that? I can tell you right now, just laying here, I feel like I fell off a fifty- foot wall .” “Humans adapt. Your bodies change in response to the way you use them .” “Hmm.” Lara closed her eyes. The heat of the fire was amazing on her skin; her last waking memory was of biting cold. “Where are we ?” “One of the few intact buildings out here. Used to house machinery of some sort, I think, but it’s been picked clean over the years. It cuts the wind, at least .” Opening her eyes, Lara examined the place. The ceiling and walls were constructed of metal sheets, parts of it rusted. Wind whistled through the holes eaten in the metal walls, and rays of sunlight streamed through holes in the roof, which meant the sun was overhead. Already midday ? “How long are we staying ?” “Not much longer. You slept for four hours and thirty-seven minutes. We need to get moving soon .” “I was afraid you’d say that.” Four hours wasn’t nearly enough. Dropping her hands, she pushed herself up. Every muscle screamed. Even after days spent scavenging or dancing, she’d never been so exhausted . “Once we reach another settlement, you’ll have time to recover .” “Will it have a bed, like your house?” She could hope, right? She’d spent all her life sleeping on the ground. Less than two weeks in Ronin’s house had spoiled her . “You should eat before we go .” Lara sighed, reaching for her bag. “On it.” She opened it up, removed a strip of dried meat, and took a bite. “Do you think they’ll follow us?” she asked around the mouthful . “It wouldn’t make sense. I broke a rule, and he said to get rid of you. That’s what I’m doing .” Lara frowned. “ What rule ?” “Humans are not to be kept by bots unless he approves of it .” She swallowed her food, and her stomach clenched around it. “That’s…that’s what happened to Tabitha, isn’t it ?” Ronin shifted his gaze to the fire and was silent for several long moments. “ Think so .” “Her bot didn’t get rid of her when he was told. So, Warlord did .” “Or the bot wasn’t deemed valuable enough to the community to earn a warning .” “But you were .” “I brought in materials that Cheyenne requires to remain prosperous. That means something to him .” Lara ran her hand over her hair, sweeping back the strands that had come out of her braid. “He’ll be angry .” “It’ll spare him the frustration of having to deal with me.” He smirked. “He didn’t care for my disregard for the rules, or my disrespectfulness .” Ronin was probably right, but she couldn’t shake the dread that had planted itself in her belly. “Why can’t bots keep humans ?” “Because Warlord hates humans .” “Yeah, I know. Even before you told me about the journal. I wish you would’ve done that sooner, by the way .” “What good would it have done , Lara ?” None. Only more fear could have come from knowing. How could she have stood at the window and looked out over that park without thinking of people dying there, if he’d told her? She’d been afraid enough of gearheads discovering her without knowing they’d killed the people who used to live in those houses. She shifted her gaze to the dwindling fire and chewed her food . “Why does he hate us so much?” she asked, after a while . “I don’t know, Lara.” Ronin shook his head, pressing his lips together. “I don’t know.
